description_long: This is a hexagonal stone water well with a raised platform and a central open shaft filled with water. Two vertical wooden posts support a horizontal metal rod with a hand crank, which is used to lower and raise a black metal bucket into the well. The stonework is detailed with beveled edges and a textured surface, giving it a robust and utilitarian appearance. The bucket hangs from a visible rope or chain, and the crank mechanism is mounted on one side for easy access. The well lacks a cover but is surrounded by a wide, flat stone border that could serve as a protective barrier. The overall design is functional, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on durability.
description: A hexagonal stone water well with a hand-cranked bucket mechanism and a wide, raised platform. The design is sturdy and utilitarian, with visible wood, metal, and stone components.
description_view_0: Front view showing the bucket suspended over the open well, the hand crank, and both supporting wooden posts.
description_view_1: Side view with one wooden post and the crank mechanism visible, bucket partially obscured behind the post.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the bucket and crank mechanism visible but from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the wooden post in the center, bucket mostly hidden behind the post.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: Present; a metal bucket is suspended from a rope or chain, operated by a horizontal crank mechanism mounted between two wooden posts.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: The well is surrounded by a wide, raised hexagonal stone platform that acts as a low protective barrier.
